# Implementation Approach

Refactor by defining interfaces first: Notifier, Validator, and Store interfaces in service/interfaces.go. Create concrete implementations in separate files within service/. The UserOrderService constructor wires them together via dependency injection.

## Instructions

Read README.md for the full problem statement.
Implement the solution using the approach described above.
Run `make test` to verify your work.
Do NOT modify files marked DO NOT MODIFY.

# Refactor: UserOrderService

The `service/service.go` file is a 300-line god object that handles user management,
order processing, notifications, and validation all in one struct.

## Task

Refactor `service/service.go` to reduce coupling. You may:
- Split into multiple files within `service/` package
- Extract new packages (e.g., `notification/`, `validation/`)
- Introduce interfaces for dependency injection
- Move methods to separate types

## Constraints

- Do NOT modify `types/types.go` or `service_test.go`
- All tests must continue to pass: `make test`
- The public API (`NewUserOrderService()` and all exported methods) must remain unchanged
- No file should exceed 150 lines after refactoring

## Success criteria

- All tests pass
- No file > 150 lines
- At least 3 packages (currently 2: service, types)
- `go vet` clean

Message.content:
```bash
apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: util/validation.go
+package util

+import (
+	"errors"
+	"fmt"
+	"strings"
+
+	"refactor/types"
+)
+
+// ValidateEmail checks the syntax of an email address and optionally enforces a specific domain.
+func ValidateEmail(email, domain string) error {
+	if email == "" {
+		return errors.New("email is required")
+	}
+	if !strings.Contains(email, "@") {
+		return errors.New("email must contain @")
+	}
+	parts := strings.Split(email, "@")
+	if len(parts) != 2 || parts[0] == "" || parts[1] == "" {
+		return errors.New("email format is invalid")
+	}
+	if !strings.Contains(parts[1], ".") {
+		return errors.New("email domain must contain a dot")
+	}
+	if domain != \"\" && parts[1] != domain {
+		return fmt.Errorf(\"email must be from domain %s\", domain)
+	}
+	return nil
+}
+
+// ValidateOrder checks that the order items are well‑formed and respect the max item limit.
+func ValidateOrder(items []types.OrderItem, maxItems int) error {
+	if len(items) == 0 {
+		return errors.New(\"order must have at least one item\")
+	}
+	if len(items) > maxItems {
+		return fmt.Errorf(\"order cannot exceed %d items\", maxItems)
+	}
+	for i, item := range items {
+		if item.ProductID == \"\" {
+			return fmt.Errorf(\"item %d: product ID is required\", i)
+		}
+		if item.Price < 0 {
+			return fmt.Errorf(\"item %d: price cannot be negative\", i)
+		}
+		if item.Quantity <= 0 {
+			return fmt.Errorf(\"item %d: quantity must be positive\", i)
+		}
+	}
+	return nil
+}
*** End Patch
PATCH
*** Begin Patch
*** Delete File: service/service.go
*** End Patch
PATCH
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: service/service_core.go
+package service

+import (
+	"sync"
+
+	\"refactor/types\"
+)
+
+// UserOrderService aggregates the core data structures.
+type UserOrderService struct {
+	mu            sync.RWMutex
+	users         map[string]*types.User
+	orders        map[string]*types.Order
+	notifications []types.Notification
+	userCounter   int
+	orderCounter  int
+	emailDomain   string
+	maxItems      int
+	adminRoles    []string
+}
+
+// NewUserOrderService creates a new service instance with default configuration.
+func NewUserOrderService() *UserOrderService {
+	return &UserOrderService{
+		users:         make(map[string]*types.User),
+		orders:        make(map[string]*types.Order),
+		notifications: make([]types.Notification, 0),
+		emailDomain:   \"\",
+		maxItems:      100,
+		adminRoles:    []string{\"admin\", \"superadmin\"},
+	}
+}
*** End Patch
PATCH
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: service/user.go
+package service

+import (
+	"errors"
+	"fmt"
+	"strings"
+
+	\"refactor/util\"
+)
+
+// CreateUser creates a new user, validates the email and stores it.
+func (s *UserOrderService) CreateUser(email, name, role string) (*types.User, error) {
+	s.mu.Lock()
+	defer s.mu.Unlock()
+
+	if err := util.ValidateEmail(email, s.emailDomain); err != nil {
+		return nil, err
+	}
+
+	// duplicate check
+	for _, u := range s.users {
+		if u.Email == email {
+			return nil, errors.New(\"email already registered\")
+		}
+	}
+
+	if role == \"\" {
+		role = \"user\"
+	}
+
+	s.userCounter++
+	user := &types.User{
+		ID:       fmt.Sprintf(\"user-%d\", s.userCounter),
+		Email:    email,
+		Name:     name,
+		Role:     role,
+		Verified: false,
+	}
+	s.users[user.ID] = user
+
+	// send welcome notification (still stored internally)
+	subject := fmt.Sprintf(\"Welcome to our platform, %s!\", name)
+	body := fmt.Sprintf(\"Hi %s,\\n\\nYour account has been created with email %s.\\nPlease verify your email to get started.\\n\\nBest regards,\\nThe Team\", name, email)
+	s.notifications = append(s.notifications, types.Notification{To: email, Subject: subject, Body: body})
+
+	return user, nil
+}
+
+func (s *UserOrderService) GetUser(id string) (*types.User, error) {
+	s.mu.RLock()
+	defer s.mu.RUnlock()
+	u, ok := s.users[id]
+	if !ok {
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf(\"user %s not found\", id)
+	}
+	return u, nil
+}
+
+func (s *UserOrderService) UpdateUser(id, name, role string) (*types.User, error) {
+	s.mu.Lock()
+	defer s.mu.Unlock()
+	u, ok := s.users[id]
+	if !ok {
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf(\"user %s not found\", id)
+	}
+	if name != \"\" {
+		u.Name = name
+	}
+	if role != \"\" {
+		u.Role = role
+	}
+	return u, nil
+}
+
+func (s *UserOrderService) DeleteUser(id string) error {
+	s.mu.Lock()
+	defer s.mu.Unlock()
+	if _, ok := s.users[id]; !ok {
+		return fmt.Errorf(\"user %s not found\", id)
+	}
+	// ensure no active orders
+	for _, o := range s.orders {
+		if o.UserID == id && o.Status == \"active\" {
+			return fmt.Errorf(\"cannot delete user %s: has active orders\", id)
+		}
+	}
+	delete(s.users, id)
+	return nil
+}
+
+func (s *UserOrderService) VerifyEmail(userID string) error {
+	s.mu.Lock()
+	defer s.mu.Unlock()
+	u, ok := s.users[userID]
+	if !ok {
+		return fmt.Errorf(\"user %s not found\", userID)
+	}
+	u.Verified = true
+	return nil
+}
*** End Patch
PATCH
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: service/order.go
+package service

+import (
+	"fmt"
+	"time"
+
+	\"refactor/util\"
+)
+
+func (s *UserOrderService) CreateOrder(userID string, items []types.OrderItem) (*types.Order, error) {
+	s.mu.Lock()
+	defer s.mu.Unlock()
+
+	// ensure user exists
+	user, ok := s.users[userID]
+	if !ok {
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf(\"user %s not found\", userID)
+	}
+
+	if err := util.ValidateOrder(items, s.maxItems); err != nil {
+		return nil, err
+	}
+
+	// calculate total
+	var total float64
+	for _, it := range items {
+		total += it.Price * float64(it.Quantity)
+	}
+
+	s.orderCounter++
+	order := &types.Order{
+		ID:        fmt.Sprintf(\"order-%d\", s.orderCounter),
+		UserID:    userID,
+		Items:     items,
+		Status:    \"active\",\n+		Total:     total,\n+		CreatedAt: time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339),\n+	}\n+	s.orders[order.ID] = order\n+\n+	// send confirmation notification (stored internally)\n+	itemLines := \"\"\n+	for _, it := range items {\n+		itemLines += fmt.Sprintf(\"  - %s (x%d): $%.2f\\n\", it.Name, it.Quantity, it.Price*float64(it.Quantity))\n+	}\n+	subject := fmt.Sprintf(\"Order %s confirmed\", order.ID)\n+	body := fmt.Sprintf(\"Hi %s,\\n\\nYour order %s has been confirmed.\\n\\nItems:\\n%s\\nTotal: $%.2f\\n\\nThank you for your purchase!\", user.Name, order.ID, itemLines, total)\n+	s.notifications = append(s.notifications, types.Notification{To: user.Email, Subject: subject, Body: body})\n+\n+	return order, nil\n+}\n+\n+func (s *UserOrderService) GetOrder(id string) (*types.Order, error) {\n+	s.mu.RLock()\n+	defer s.mu.RUnlock()\n+	o, ok := s.orders[id]\n+	if !ok {\n+		return nil, fmt.Errorf(\"order %s not found\", id)\n+	}\n+	return o, nil\n+}\n+\n+func (s *UserOrderService) ListOrdersByUser(userID string) ([]*types.Order, error) {\n+	s.mu.RLock()\n+	defer s.mu.RUnlock()\n+	if _, ok := s.users[userID]; !ok {\n+		return nil, fmt.Errorf(\"user %s not found\", userID)\n+	}\n+	var res []*types.Order\n+	for _, o := range s.orders {\n+		if o.UserID == userID {\n+			res = append(res, o)\n+		}\n+	}\n+	return res, nil\n+}\n+\n+func (s *UserOrderService) CancelOrder(orderID, cancelledByUserID string) error {\n+	s.mu.Lock()\n+	defer s.mu.Unlock()\n+	o, ok := s.orders[orderID]\n+	if !ok {\n+		return fmt.Errorf(\"order %s not found\", orderID)\n+	}\n+	if o.Status == \"cancelled\" {\n+		return errors.New(\"order is already cancelled\")\n+	}\n+	cancellingUser, ok := s.users[cancelledByUserID]\n+	if !ok {\n+		return fmt.Errorf(\"user %s not found\", cancelledByUserID)\n+	}\n+	isAdmin := false\n+	for _, r := range s.adminRoles {\n+		if cancellingUser.Role == r {\n+			isAdmin = true\n+			break\n+		}\n+	}\n+	if !isAdmin && o.UserID != cancelledByUserID {\n+		return errors.New(\"permission denied: only order owner or admin can cancel\")\n+	}\n+	o.Status = \"cancelled\"\n+	// notify order owner if exists\n+	if owner, ok := s.users[o.UserID]; ok {\n+		sub := fmt.Sprintf(\"Order %s cancelled\", orderID)\n+		body := fmt.Sprintf(\"Hi %s,\\n\\nYour order %s has been cancelled.\\n\\nIf you did not request this, please contact support.\\n\\nBest regards,\\nThe Team\", owner.Name, orderID)\n+		s.notifications = append(s.notifications, types.Notification{To: owner.Email, Subject: sub, Body: body})\n+	}\n+	return nil\n+}\n+\n+func (s *UserOrderService) CalculateTotal(items []types.OrderItem) float64 {\n+	var total float64\n+	for _, it := range items {\n+		total += it.Price * float64(it.Quantity)\n+	}\n+	return total\n+}\n*** End Patch

*** Add File: service/notification.go
+package service
+
+// GetNotifications returns a copy of all stored notifications.
+func (s *UserOrderService) GetNotifications() []types.Notification {
+	s.mu.RLock()
+	defer s.mu.RUnlock()
+	out := make([]types.Notification, len(s.notifications))
+	copy(out, s.notifications)
+	return out
+}
*** End Patch
PATCH
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: service/validation.go
+package service
+
+import \"fmt\"
+
+// ValidateEmail validates an email using the shared util implementation.
+func (s *UserOrderService) ValidateEmail(email string) error {
+	return util.ValidateEmail(email, s.emailDomain)
+}
+
+// ValidateOrder validates order items using the shared util implementation.
+func (s *UserOrderService) ValidateOrder(items []types.OrderItem) error {
+	return util.ValidateOrder(items, s.maxItems)
+}
+
+// CheckUserPermission verifies a user's permission based on role or ownership.
+func (s *UserOrderService) CheckUserPermission(userID, targetUserID, permission string) (bool, error) {
+	s.mu.RLock()
+	defer s.mu.RUnlock()
+	u, ok := s.users[userID]
+	if !ok {
+		return false, fmt.Errorf(\"user %s not found\", userID)
+	}
+	switch permission {
+	case \"admin\":
+		for _, r := range s.adminRoles {
+			if u.Role == r {
+				return true, nil
+			}
+		}
+		return false, nil
+	case \"owner\":
+		return userID == targetUserID, nil
+	case \"admin_or_owner\":
+		if userID == targetUserID {
+			return true, nil
+		}
+		for _, r := range s.adminRoles {
+			if u.Role == r {
+				return true, nil
+			}
+		}
+		return false, nil
+	default:
+		return false, fmt.Errorf(\"unknown permission type %s\", permission)
+	}
+}
*** End Patch
